description: E2E test case designs for opencode-storage crate - SQLite persistence layer

test_cases.title: Session save and load roundtrip
test_cases.description: Test session persists to SQLite and loads correctly

test_cases.title: Crash recovery for incomplete sessions
test_cases.description: Test recovery of sessions from interrupted operations
steps: Create session with in_progress tool invocation
expected_outcomes: Recovery completes or marks as failed

test_cases.title: Session compaction
test_cases.description: Test compaction reduces session size while preserving summary

test_cases.title: Concurrent session access
test_cases.description: Test multiple concurrent operations on different sessions

test_cases.title: Database migration
steps: Run migrations again on existing DB

test_cases.title: Shareability verification
test_cases.description: Test CompactionManager verifies shareability before compaction

test_cases.title: CRITICAL: SQLite database corruption recovery
test_cases.description: System must handle corrupted SQLite database gracefully
test_cases.risk_addressed: Data loss and process crash on corrupted SQLite file
steps: Write corrupted bytes to SQLite file (randomize header)
Verify error is descriptive (SqliteError, not panic)
expected_outcomes: Storage open returns error, not panic
post_checks: Can create fresh storage in new location
No data loss from uncorrupted sessions

test_cases.title: Disk full during write
test_cases.description: Storage must handle disk full errors during session save
test_cases.risk_addressed: Data loss when disk fills mid-write
steps: Simulate disk full (quota or filesystem full)
post_checks: Storage still functional for other sessions
Can retry save after space freed

test_cases.title: CRITICAL: Concurrent writes to same session
test_cases.description: Concurrent saves to same session must not corrupt data
test_cases.risk_addressed: Data corruption from concurrent writes to same session
steps: Spawn 10 threads all modifying session A simultaneously
Verify data is consistent (not partially overwritten)
expected_outcomes: Final state is one of the written values (not corruption)

test_cases.title: Concurrent session deletion while reading
test_cases.description: Delete session while other operations are in-flight
steps: Verify either succeeds with data or returns not-found
expected_outcomes: Error is NotFound or data returned correctly

test_cases.title: Migration rollback on failure
test_cases.description: Failed migration must not leave schema in broken state
test_cases.risk_addressed: Database stuck in invalid schema after failed migration
steps: Apply migrations up to version N+1
Simulate failure during N+2 migration (corrupt migration file)
Verify database still works at version N+1
expected_outcomes: Schema remains at last valid version

test_cases.title: Large migration with many sessions
test_cases.description: Migration performance and atomicity with large data
test_cases.risk_addressed: Migration takes too long or leaves partial state
steps: Run migration that alters session schema
expected_outcomes: Crash recovery does not repeat completed work

test_cases.title: Session list pagination
test_cases.description: list_sessions must support pagination for large session counts
test_cases.risk_addressed: Memory exhaustion when listing thousands of sessions

test_cases.title: Session search by metadata
test_cases.description: Search sessions by criteria (date range, agent type, etc.)
steps: Create sessions with different agent types and dates

test_cases.title: Online backup while active
test_cases.description: Backup can run while storage is accepting writes
test_cases.risk_addressed: Backup of active database may be inconsistent
steps: While backup running, save new sessions
Verify backup is valid SQLite file
Verify backup contains recent data OR is point-in-time consistent

critical_invariants: Corrupted database does not crash process
Concurrent writes to same session are serialized safely
Disk full does not corrupt existing data
high_risk_scenarios: storage_migr_001: Migration failure leaves DB unusable
